name: Reload
description: A committed action that transfers ammunition into a weapon before it can fire again.
design_notes: Reload turns ranged pressure into a pacing loop. It should define partial magazines, interruption, tactical reloads, animation sync, and what happens when inventory ammo is low.
design_purpose: Create downtime and vulnerability for ranged attacks.
Support weapon identity through magazine and reload behavior.
player_fantasy: The player feels the tension of preparing a weapon while danger continues around them.

parameters.name: magazine_capacity
parameters.description: Rounds held by the active weapon magazine.

parameters.name: reload_commit_frame
parameters.description: Frame where ammunition is actually transferred.

parameters.name: rounds_loaded_per_insert
parameters.description: Rounds restored per shell, clip, or magazine insert step.

parameters.name: reload_cancel_refund_rule
parameters.description: How canceling reload affects ammo state.

edge_cases: Reload interrupted by sprint, damage, weapon swap, or cutscene needs ammo transfer rules.
Save/load, reconnect, or rollback during reload must restore reload_started_at_ms, commit state, magazine count, reserve count, and cancel policy together.
Per-shell or staged reloads need rules for partial completion when reserve ammo runs out mid-sequence.
Client prediction must not duplicate ammo on cancel.
Auto-reload at zero ammo can conflict with manual reload input, buffered firing, or weapon-swap input in the same tick.
Death, stun, weapon drop, or equipment removal after the commit frame but before the animation ends needs a clear final ammo state.
common_bugs: Ammo is added at animation start and duplicated after cancel.
Reload finishes visually but weapon state remains unable to fire.
Inventory reserve can go negative after rapid weapon switching.
Firing is accepted on the same frame as reload cancel because weapon_state changes before the ammo transaction resolves.
Saving during reload restores magazine and reserve counts but not reload progress, allowing a free instant reload after load.
Ammo UI shows predicted loaded rounds after the server rejects reload because reserve ammo, range, or weapon ownership changed.
balancing_notes: Long reload windows create tactical exposure; short reloads make ammo mostly cosmetic.
Per-round reloads are more flexible and should cost less total vulnerability per partial refill.
accessibility_notes: Use clear magazine and reserve indicators and avoid relying only on click sounds for empty weapons.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent reload as weapon_state with reload_started_at_ms, commit_frame, cancel_policy, magazine_count, reserve_count, and transaction_id. Transfer ammo only at configured commit points, then reconcile animation events, UI, and save/load from that authoritative state.
implementation_notes.godot: Store magazine and reserve counts on the weapon resource or node, drive reload timers from _physics_process, and apply per-shell inserts from explicit state events. Clear or persist reload_state deliberately when changing scenes, swapping weapons, or losing player control.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement reload in the weapon or Gameplay Ability state machine, using montage notifies for visual commit cues while keeping ammo authority on the server. Replicate reload_state, commit index, and reserve deltas so prediction and reconnect do not duplicate ammunition.
implementation_notes.web: Model reload as a timed weapon state with reload_started_ms, commit frame, cancel policy, and ammo transaction id. Update magazine and reserve counts in one deterministic transaction before rendering UI or accepting buffered fire input.

relationships.reason: Reload needs a reliable timing window before the next valid firing action is accepted.
Reload restores firing availability and should share ammo authority with ranged attack validation.

example_games.title: Counter-Strike 2
example_games.note: Uses reload commitment as a tactical timing cost in firefights.

example_games.title: Gears of War
example_games.note: Uses reload timing, including active reload feedback, as a visible combat pacing element.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
example_games.note: Uses reload timing and ammunition pressure in firefights.

example_games.title: Resident Evil 4
example_games.note: Uses reload timing and ammunition pressure as part of combat pacing.

example_games.title: Fortnite
example_games.note: Uses reload timing and magazine limits during ranged combat.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses reload timing for many ranged heroes and weapons.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses reload timing and magazine limits during squad firefights.

example_games.title: Left 4 Dead
example_games.note: Uses reload timing and ammunition pressure during horde encounters.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses reload timing and ammunition pressure during first-person firefights.
